_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence finalization, City of Savannah GA batch 2: Mayor Van Johnson + aldermen Wilder-Bryan (D3), Palumbo (D4), Shabazz (D5), Purtee (D6). Parallel web research June 10, 2026; rosters verified against savannahga.gov + 2026 council action minutes. Local tier; social-media/minutes protocol. Conventions consistent with natl-local-GA-2026-06-09 batches: unanimous April 2024 guns-in-unlocked-cars ordinance = self_defense q1 FALSE (verified 9-0-0 minutes); mayor-presented body-level proclamations without individual advocacy = null; 'publicly backed police' requires affirmative advocacy beyond routine budget votes.

profile.education: BBA, Savannah State University; MPA, Georgia Southern University
profile.background: Brooklyn NY native; former Chatham County police officer and county HR administrator; Savannah alderman 2004-2019; 67th mayor since 2020, re-elected Nov 2023 with 77%; elected 2nd Vice President of the National League of Cities (Dec 2024)

van-johnson.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: sitting mayor June 2026 (savannahga.gov/388; WTOC weekly presser 1/20/2026); no run for other office found. Term ends 2027. HARD evidence: pro-choice in policy - 'Who am I to determine the physical being of a woman?', called Dobbs 'a huge step backwards' (WSAV 2022) though says he personally 'leans more toward pro-life'; presided over July 2022 council resolution supporting reproductive rights - life q0/q1 FALSE. Created PROUD Savannah LGBTQ+ taskforce (2020), Pride Month + Progress flag in City Hall, Transgender Day of Remembrance posts - marriage q2/q4 FALSE (no marriage-definition statement found, q0 null). Affirmative police advocacy: pushed SPD starting pay to $60,350, $1.5M raise/bonus package; ex-cop; no defund rhetoric - public_justice q0 TRUE. Signed GA's first municipal mask mandate (6/2020), fought Kemp to keep mandate powers ('Kemp does not give a damn about us'), urged churches to stay closed spring 2020 - was the mandate-imposer, not the protector from mandates - overreach q2 FALSE; but consistently affirms local decision-making authority vs the state (mask fight 2020; defending city gun ordinance vs AG preemption 2025) - subsidiarity q3 TRUE. Championed April 2024 unlocked-car gun ordinance (struck down 11/2025 as preempted) + gun buybacks - self_defense q1 FALSE. NULLs explained: protested SB 202 broadly but no specific photo-voter-ID position (election q1 null); 'We're not in the immigration business' is neither sanctuary nor anti-sanctuary (border null); crime framing mixes 'whole community' language with targeted policing - no avoid-punishment framing (pj q3 null). 'He is a pastor' claim NOT verified - no clergy role found; church membership unconfirmed (religion null). 2025 millage rolled back to lowest since 1987 after 2024 hold above rollback rate - mixed, econ q2 null. Score ~25/100 from 2 TRUE / 6 FALSE; nulls don't penalize.

0.note: No conception/personhood affirmation; documented pro-choice policy stance - 'Men should not make decisions about the disposition of women's bodies,' called overturning Roe 'a huge step backwards' (2022), though saying he personally 'leans more toward pro-life.'

1.note: Presided over the July 2022 council resolution supporting women's reproductive rights - documented advocacy contrary to abortion abolition.

2.note: Affirms gender-identity ideology: Transgender Day of Remembrance post (Nov 2024); founded PROUD Savannah welcoming residents 'no matter... how they identify, or how they express themselves'; city revised bathroom ordinances for gender-non-conforming residents.

4.note: Created the city's official PROUD Savannah LGBTQ+ taskforce (July 2020); city recognizes Pride Month with the Progress flag displayed in the City Hall rotunda - active promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine budgets: championed raising SPD starting pay to $60,350 via a $1.5M package with 5% officer raises and $100K hiring bonuses; former police officer; no defund rhetoric found.

2.note: Was the COVID mandate-imposer, not the protector from mandates: signed Georgia's first municipal mask mandate (June 2020), fought to retain local mandate powers, and urged houses of worship to keep their doors closed (spring 2020).

3.note: Consistently affirms local decision-making vs the state: 'we have the ability... the responsibility and... the authority' (2020, vs Kemp) and defends the city's ordinance against the AG's preemption suit (2025). Credit for the subsidiarity principle, noting he wields local control toward mandates and gun restrictions.

1.note: Championed the April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles (up to $1,000/30 days; struck down Nov 2025 as state-preempted), still defends it publicly (Jan 2026), and pushed gun buyback programs.

profile.background: Savannah native; ~20-year law-enforcement career (sheriff's office sergeant); later worked under DA Meg Heap; entered politics after her son Lawrence Bryan IV was murdered in 2015, co-founding the LB4 & After Foundation; nonprofit affordable-housing work (Dundee Cottages); elected 2019, re-elected 2023 with 55.8%

linda-wilder-bryan.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 3, present in Jan 22, 2026 action minutes; council Vice Chair confirmed through Dec 2025 minutes (title absent from Jan 2026 minutes - 2026 status unconfirmed). Son's name is Lawrence Bryan IV (not 'LaVar' as in some prior data). HARD evidence: recognized Rev. Timothy Sheppard (Central Missionary Baptist) to offer the council invocation, Feb 2025 minutes - christian_liberty q3 TRUE. Backed SB 92 (GA prosecutor-oversight commission): 'I stood with Meg Heap because they made a trial out of a case for my son,' criticizing the sitting DA for pleading cases out - public_justice q1 TRUE. Vocally backed the April 2024 unlocked-car gun ordinance ('a coward had a gun who should not have had a gun'; struck down 11/2025 as preempted) + years of anti-gun-violence activism (monthly die-ins, CHAOS) - self_defense q1 FALSE. NULLs explained: 'I have 20 years of law enforcement and own two guns' + 'everybody needs to be held accountable' are background/accountability statements, not affirmative pro-police advocacy (pj q0 null) and not a constitutional-carry position (sd q0 null; says she believes in the Second Amendment). Oct 2024 Pride proclamation was an agenda-amendment vote, body-level and mayor-presented - null per batch convention. Routine unanimous budget/millage votes - econ q2 null. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage definition, trans policy, schools, elections, immigration. Faith-expressive ('Watch God, Be God') but denomination/membership unconfirmed - religion null. Score ~67/100 from 2 TRUE / 1 FALSE - sparse-data, nulls don't penalize.

3.note: Official council minutes, Feb 13, 2025: 'Alderwoman Wilder-Bryan recognized Reverend Timothy Sheppard, pastor of Central Missionary Baptist Church to offer a prayer' - supports prayer in public bodies.

1.note: Backed SB 92, Georgia's prosecutor-oversight/removal commission aimed at DAs who under-prosecute: 'I stood with Meg Heap because they made a trial out of a case for my son,' criticizing the current DA for pleading cases out (Oct 2023).

Vocally backed the April 2024 ordinance fining unsecured guns in unlocked cars ('a coward had a gun who should not have had a gun'; struck down Nov 2025 as preempted); longtime anti-gun-violence activism including monthly die-ins.

profile.education: B.S. Administrative Science, University of South Alabama; M.Ed., Armstrong State University
profile.background: New Orleans native; former U.S. Foreign Service special agent (Meritorious Honor Award, US Embassy Baghdad) and U.S. Park Police officer; neighborhood-association president and urbanism/housing advocate; District 4 alderman since 2020; Council Chairman since Jan 2024

nick-palumbo.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 4, term Jan 2024-Dec 2027, Council Chairman since Jan 2024 (savannahga.gov/3031; Granicus board records); no run for higher office - instead endorsed Geoff Duncan for governor 2026. HARD evidence: publicly championed the 2025 millage rollback to the lowest rate since 1987: 'we're rolling back the millage rates to cut taxes... give back to our community' - econ q2 TRUE. Advocated raising police pay: cites 'raising police salaries' among priorities (WTOC 2025); 2023 campaign profile backed raising new-officer compensation above $50,000; former federal law enforcement - public_justice q0 TRUE. Voted YES (9-0-0 verified minutes) on the April 2024 unlocked-car gun ordinance, struck down 11/2025 as preempted - self_defense q1 FALSE. NULLs explained: July 2022 reproductive-rights resolution passed unanimously but outlets published no roll call and no Palumbo statement found - life null per body-level convention; PROUD Savannah taskforce and Pride actions were mayoral - no Palumbo-specific marriage/gender evidence, null; Roger Moss school-board endorsement is not a rubric position. NO EVIDENCE on schools policy, Christian liberty, elections, immigration, state overreach (no personal local-control statement). Religion: no evidence. Score ~67/100 from 2 TRUE / 1 FALSE - sparse-data, nulls don't penalize.

2.note: Affirmative tax-cut advocacy, July 2025: voted for and publicly championed rolling the millage back to the lowest rate since 1987 - 'we're rolling back the millage rates to cut taxes' and 'give back to our community.'

0.note: Affirmative advocacy for police pay: names 'raising police salaries' among city priorities (2025); 2023 campaign profile supported raising new-officer compensation above $50,000/yr; former U.S. Foreign Service special agent and U.S. Park Police officer. No defund rhetoric found.

1.note: Voted YES (unanimous 9-0-0, official minutes) on the April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles; struck down Nov 2025 as preempted by Georgia law.

set.office: City Council Alderwoman, District 5 (Mayor Pro Tem)

profile.religion: Christian — founder and chief bishop, New Afrikan Methodist Christian Church (ordained AME deacon/elder; M.Div., D.Min.)
profile.education: B.S. Civil Engineering Technology, Savannah State University; M.Div., Turner Theological Seminary; D.Min., United Theological Seminary
profile.background: First Black woman civil engineer hired by the City of Savannah (1986); 40+ year entrepreneur (restaurant, newspaper, transportation); District 5 alderwoman since 2012 (first elected 2011, 4th term, longest-serving); Mayor Pro Tem; mentored by Dr. Betty Shabazz

estella-shabazz.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 5 + Mayor Pro Tem, current June 2026 (savannahga.gov/3032; Feb 12, 2026 action minutes). 4th term, first elected 2011. The 'Nation of Islam' surname assumption is affirmatively disproven: she is an ordained AME deacon/elder who founded her own Christian denomination (New Afrikan Methodist Christian Church) and serves as chief bishop; city-published profile professes 'Seek ye first the kingdom of God'; National Chaplain, NLC National Black Caucus - christian_liberty q0 TRUE. Police Benevolent Association + Savannah Firefighters endorsements in 2023 race (single-issue LE endorsement) and urged getting 'guns and those who use guns to harm others off of our streets' - public_justice q0 TRUE. Voted YES (9-0-0 verified minutes) on the April 2024 unlocked-car gun ordinance, struck down 11/2025 as preempted - self_defense q1 FALSE. NULLs explained: welcoming remarks at VP Harris's 2024 reproductive-rights tour stop were hospitality, not a policy position - life null; Oct 2024 Pride proclamation entered via unanimous agenda-amendment vote, mayor-presented, no individual advocacy - marriage null per batch convention; campaign slogan 'keeping taxes low' is vague + routine unanimous millage votes - econ q2 null. NO EVIDENCE on schools, elections, immigration, state overreach (mask/gun votes were body-level; overreach not credited per convention). Score ~67/100 from 2 TRUE / 1 FALSE - sparse-data, nulls don't penalize.

0.note: Public profession of Christ while in office: ordained AME deacon/elder, M.Div./D.Min., founder and chief bishop of the New Afrikan Methodist Christian Church; city-published profile professes 'Seek ye first the kingdom of God'; served as National Chaplain of the NLC National Black Caucus of Local Elected Officials.

Endorsed by the Police Benevolent Association and Savannah Firefighters Association in her 2023 race (single-issue law-enforcement endorsements = hard evidence of backing police); publicly urged getting 'guns and those who use guns to harm others off of our streets' (2020). No defund advocacy found.

1.note: Voted YES (unanimous 9-0-0, official minutes, present and voting) on the April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles; struck down Nov 2025 as preempted by Georgia law.

profile.religion: Member, Asbury Memorial Church, Savannah (chairs its Safety Ministry)
profile.education: B.S. Criminal Justice Administration; M.S. Criminal Justice, Georgia Southern University
profile.background: Michigan native; 21+ year law-enforcement career (police officer at 19, Garden City PD detective, Georgia Southern University PD captain); first openly gay Savannah council member; elected 2019, 2nd term

kurtis-purtee.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 6, 2nd term Jan 2024-Dec 2027 (savannahga.gov/3033). HARD evidence: accepted LGBTQ Victory Fund (2019) and Georgia Equality PAC endorsements - single-issue LGBTQ-equality endorsements; first openly gay Savannah council member; serves on PROUD Savannah, the city's official LGBTQIA+ taskforce, credited with raising the city's Municipal Equality Index score - marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. July 2023: voted with the majority to keep the 12.20 millage rather than take the full rollback, defending the revenue need ('We do not have a fund decided for stormwater') - econ q2 FALSE (2025 rollback vote unreported individually). Affirmative pro-police statement amid SPD morale controversy: 'I support our police officers and I support accountability' (4/2022) + 21-yr LE career - public_justice q0 TRUE. Unanimous YES (9-0-0 minutes) on April 2024 unlocked-car gun ordinance, struck down 11/2025 - self_defense q1 FALSE. NULLs explained: behavioral-health-advocates-per-precinct and prevention framing concern mental-health calls and youth, not avoiding punishment for violent crime (pj q3 null); acknowledges Savannah 'has a gang problem.' NO EVIDENCE on abortion, schools, elections, immigration, state overreach. Current employer ambiguity (GSU PD captain per city bio vs school-system police per LinkedIn) unresolved. Conduct note on record: 2021 bodycam slur exchange with a colleague, publicly regretted. Score ~17/100 from 1 TRUE / 5 FALSE.

0.note: Accepted endorsements from the LGBTQ Victory Fund and Georgia Equality PAC - single-issue organizations whose core mission includes marriage equality; first openly gay Savannah council member. Documented contrary position to exclusively man-woman marriage.

2.note: Serves on PROUD Savannah, the city's official LGBTQIA+ taskforce 'dedicated to ensuring that its lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer communities can work and live in a welcoming and safe environment' - affirms rather than rejects gender-identity ideology.

4.note: Active promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in city policy: serves on the city's official LGBTQIA+ taskforce and is credited with helping raise Savannah's Municipal Equality Index score.

2.note: July 2023: voted with the majority to keep the millage at 12.20 rather than take the full rollback (declining the tax reduction; two members opposed), defending the revenue need: 'We do not have a fund decided for stormwater.' (Council later rolled back to 11.75 in 2025; his individual vote unreported.)

0.note: Affirmative public backing amid an SPD morale/departure controversy: 'I support our police officers and I support accountability' (April 2022); 21+ year law-enforcement career, university PD captain. No defund rhetoric found.

1.note: Voted YES (unanimous 9-0-0, official minutes) on the April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles; struck down Nov 2025 as preempted by Georgia law.
